# Icon Assets Guide

## 📁 Where to Add Icons

### Option 1: Icons in Your Application (Recommended)

**DO NOT include icons in the npm package.** Instead, users should add icons to their own application.

Icons should be placed in the **public folder** of your Angular application:

```
your-app/
├── public/
│   └── assets/
│       └── icons/
│           ├── Line/
│           │   ├── 10px/
│           │   │   ├── home.svg
│           │   │   ├── user.svg
│           │   │   └── settings.svg
│           │   ├── 16px/
│           │   │   ├── home.svg
│           │   │   ├── user.svg
│           │   │   └── settings.svg
│           │   ├── 20px/
│           │   │   ├── home--20.svg
│           │   │   ├── user--20.svg
│           │   │   └── settings--20.svg
│           │   ├── 24px/
│           │   │   ├── home--24.svg
│           │   │   ├── user--24.svg
│           │   │   └── settings--24.svg
│           │   ├── 32px/
│           │   │   ├── home--32.svg
│           │   │   ├── user--32.svg
│           │   │   └── settings--32.svg
│           │   └── 48px/
│           │       ├── home--48.svg
│           │       ├── user--48.svg
│           │       └── settings--48.svg
│           ├── Fill/
│           │   └── [same structure as Line]
│           ├── Duotone/
│           │   └── [same structure as Line]
│           └── Colour/
│               └── [same structure as Line]
```

### Important Naming Convention

- **For 10px and 16px sizes**: Use simple name: `icon-name.svg`
  - Example: `home.svg`, `user.svg`

- **For 20px, 24px, 32px, 48px sizes**: Add size suffix: `icon-name--{size}.svg`
  - Example: `home--20.svg`, `home--24.svg`, `home--32.svg`, `home--48.svg`

## 🎨 Usage in Your App

Once icons are in the public folder:

```typescript
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { FNIconComponent } from '@arpudhabotupload/fn-icon-angular';

@Component({
  selector: 'app-root',
  standalone: true,
  imports: [FNIconComponent],
  template: `
    <fn-icon name="home" variant="Line" size="medium"></fn-icon>
  `
})
export class AppComponent {}
```

This will load: `assets/icons/Line/20px/home--20.svg`

## 🔧 Custom Icon Path

If your icons are in a different location:

```typescript
<fn-icon 
  name="home" 
  basePath="custom/path/to/icons">
</fn-icon>
```

## Option 2: Include Sample Icons in Package (For Testing)

If you want to include a few sample icons in the npm package for testing purposes:

```
fn-icon-angular/
├── public/
│   └── assets/
│       └── icons/
│           └── Line/
│               └── 24px/
│                   └── sample--24.svg
```

Then update `.npmignore` to include public folder:

```
src
tsconfig.json
.gitignore
*.log
node_modules
examples
# Remove 'public' from this list if you want to include it
```

## 📦 Publishing Recommendations

### ✅ Recommended Approach (Exclude Icons)

**Pros:**
- Smaller package size
- Users only include icons they need
- No copyright/licensing issues with icon sets
- Faster installation
- Users can use any icon library they want

**Cons:**
- Users must set up icon structure themselves
- Need to provide clear documentation

### ❌ Not Recommended (Include Icons)

Including icons in the package:

**Pros:**
- Easier for users to get started
- Plug-and-play experience

**Cons:**
- Large package size (icons can be hundreds of MB)
- Slow npm installation
- Users download icons they may never use
- Potential licensing issues
- Version updates require re-downloading all icons
- Cannot customize icon set easily

## 📝 Documentation for Users

Add this to your README:

```markdown
## Setting Up Icons

This library requires you to provide your own icon SVG files. Place icons in your `public/assets/icons/` folder following this structure:

\`\`\`
public/assets/icons/{Variant}/{Size}px/{icon-name}[--{size}].svg
\`\`\`

### Example:
- Line variant, 24px: `public/assets/icons/Line/24px/home--24.svg`
- Line variant, 16px: `public/assets/icons/Line/16px/home.svg`

### Supported Icon Libraries:
- [Iconoir](https://iconoir.com/)
- [Lucide Icons](https://lucide.dev/)
- [Heroicons](https://heroicons.com/)
- Any custom SVG icons
\`\`\`

## 🎯 Best Practice

1. **Publish package WITHOUT icons** - Keep it lightweight
2. **Provide sample project** - Create a GitHub repo with example icon setup
3. **Document clearly** - Explain icon structure in README
4. **Offer script** - Provide a script to help users organize their icons

## 🔨 Example Setup Script

Create a helper script for users:

\`\`\`javascript
// scripts/setup-icons.js
const fs = require('fs');
const path = require('path');

const variants = ['Line', 'Fill', 'Duotone', 'Colour'];
const sizes = ['10px', '16px', '20px', '24px', '32px', '48px'];

const baseDir = path.join(process.cwd(), 'public', 'assets', 'icons');

variants.forEach(variant => {
  sizes.forEach(size => {
    const dir = path.join(baseDir, variant, size);
    fs.mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true });
  });
});

console.log('✅ Icon directory structure created!');
\`\`\`
